$comment: Hand-authored content layered over the mechanical ZIL extraction. Merged by app/scripts/extract-zork.ts after the ZIL pass. Per-id deep merge for passages/rooms/items (sides matched by roomId, exits shallow-merged); customTools and triggers are appended.
$startStateComment: Initial flag values. player-health drives the player's HP for combat (drained by combat triggers; player-killed trigger ends the game when it hits zero). Boolean flags are pre-initialized to false so that `flag(key, false)` Conditions evaluate correctly — without this, an unset flag is `undefined` and `undefined === false` is false (strict equality in evaluateCondition), so 'not yet flipped' gates would silently never activate.

startState.$puzzleFlagsComment: Pre-init for the Phase-4 puzzle batch. Each is paired to its setter trigger below; pre-init avoids the unset-vs-false strict-equality gotcha when triggers gate on flag(X, false).

$darknessComment: Darkness model: a room is dark when it declares state.dark === true and no perceivable item has state.isLit === true. Composed from generic primitives (currentRoomState + anyPerceivableItemWith) — engine has no hardcoded darkness concept.

sharedVariants.text: It is pitch black. You are likely to be eaten by a grue.

$templatesComment: Build-time partial-Item templates. Items declare `fromTemplate: 'name'` to inherit fields; the extractor merges (item fields win at the leaf, arrays union, nested objects deep-merged) and strips `fromTemplate` from the final story. Engine never sees templates. Define-once, instantiate-many.

templates.$bladed-weapon-comment: Shared shape for sword, axe, knife. State keys (damage, weapon-stuck, attack-bonus-add/pct) are conventions class triggers gate on. Tags compose downstream.

passages.$comment: FRONT-DOOR. Connects west-of-house (outside) to living-room (inside). Solid oak banded with iron, barred from the inside — there's no handle on the inner face. Permanently sealed; the player must use the kitchen-window to enter the house. traversableWhen always false; per-side names so the inside view reads 'wooden door' and the outside reads 'front door'.
passages.name: front door
passages.description: Solid oak crossed with thick iron bands. There is no handle on this side and no obvious way to open it.
passages.narratorNote: This door is sealed from within — the player cannot open it from either side. It is the canonical 'barred front door'. When the player tries to open it or go through it, narrate the door's solid construction (heavy oak, iron bands, no handle, no keyhole on this side). Direct them to find another way in (the kitchen window) or out (likewise).

sides.name: front door
sides.description: The handsome front door of the white house — solid oak, iron-banded, evidently barred from within.

sides.name: wooden door
sides.description: A heavy oak door crossed with thick iron bands — the front door, barred shut from this side. No handle, no keyhole.

passages.traverseBlockedMessage: The door is barred shut from the inside — solid oak, iron-banded, no handle on this side. You'll need to find another way through.

passages.description: A small kitchen window. It appears to be slightly ajar.

variants.text: The kitchen window is open wide enough to climb through.

glimpse.prompt: Through the window, describe the area behind the house briefly — open ground sloping toward forest. Keep it to a sentence.
Through the window, describe the kitchen briefly — emphasize warmth and signs of recent cooking. Keep it to a sentence.

passages.$comment: Chimney is a CLIMBBIT/LOCAL-GLOBAL scenery in ZIL, not a DOORBIT, so mechanical extraction won't catch it. Hand-authored. Canonical Zork rules: kitchen→studio (down) is never traversable; studio→kitchen (up) requires the lamp and ≤2 carried items.
passages.name: chimney
passages.description: A dark, narrow chimney rising from the studio fireplace up to the kitchen above.

sides.traverseBlockedMessage: Only Santa Claus climbs down chimneys.
You can't get up there with what you're carrying.

passages.$comment: Trap door is HIDDEN under the rug in the living room. visibleWhen filters it out of passagesHere (so view + examine both refuse to surface it) until rug-moved-flag flips. openWhen gates the open intent: rug must be moved AND player must NOT be in the cellar (no handle on the underside — once it slams shut you can't reopen it from below; you have to loop back through the underground to the kitchen). traversableWhen gates traversal so a closed door can't be walked through.
passages.description: A wooden trap door is set into the floor.

passages.$comment: Grate is locked from above; opens only with the skeleton key (item id: keys, found in the maze near the skeleton). Symmetric — once unlocked, can open from either side. traversableWhen gates traversal on its open state.
passages.description: A heavy iron grating set into the floor — or, depending on which side you stand on, into the ceiling. Beyond it lies a clearing of scattered leaves.

rooms.$comment: Reservoir is the canonical missing room description — the ZIL routine walker didn't catch its M-LOOK branch. Default reads as the high-water state (player's first visit); low-tide variant reads as the drained mud-flat (post-dam-puzzle) and calls out the trunk treasure half-buried in the mud.
rooms.description: You are in a vast underground chamber, half-filled with cold dark water. The reservoir stretches north and south, fed from the north and drained by the dam to the south. The water laps gently at the walls.

variants.text: You are in a vast underground chamber. With the reservoir drained, the floor is a flat expanse of dark mud and silt — passable, if unpleasant. The chamber stretches north and south, fed from the north and drained by the dam to the south. Half-buried in the mud, an old wooden trunk gleams with what might be jewels.

rooms.$comment: Reservoir-south: the lake's south shore. Default = full lake (impassable to the north); low-tide variant = exposed mud-flat (north traversal opens via the existing low-tide-gated exit). Override redeclares the gated north exit so the blockedMessage attaches alongside the existing `when` gate.
rooms.description: You are in a long room on the south shore of a large lake, far too deep and wide for crossing. There is a path along the stream to the east or west, a steep pathway climbing southwest along the edge of a chasm, and a path leading into a canyon to the southeast.

variants.text: You are at the south end of what was once a large lake. With the reservoir drained, the lake bed lies exposed — a stretch of dark mud, silt, and the occasional rusted relic. A path runs east and west; a steep pathway climbs southwest along the edge of a chasm; a canyon opens to the southeast. To the north, the dry bed leads into the reservoir proper.

north.blockedMessage: The reservoir is too deep and the water far too cold — you'd drown before reaching the other side.

rooms.$comment: Reservoir-north: the head of the reservoir, fed by a stream from the north. Default = wide deep stream feeding the lake; low-tide variant = sluggish trickle through mud.
rooms.description: You are in a large cavernous room, the south of which is a wide, deep stream — the head of the reservoir. There is a slimy stairway leaving the room to the north.

variants.text: You are in a large cavernous room. The southern half, formerly a wide, deep stream feeding the reservoir, is now reduced to a sluggish trickle through mud. A slimy stairway leaves the room to the north.

rooms.$comment: kitchen.down: ZIL has CEXIT against FALSE-FLAG (a never-true sentinel) — mechanical extraction produces an unreachable flag-gated exit. Replace with a passage exit so the chimney passage's traversableWhen drives it. Description: state-neutral base; variants append the canonical window state.
rooms.description: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window.

variants.text: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is open.

You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.$comment: studio.up: ZIL has FEXIT (PER UP-CHIMNEY-FUNCTION) — skipped by mechanical extraction. Add it as a passage exit.

grating-clearing.down: ZIL extraction wired the up-direction (grating-room.up → grating-clearing) but left the reverse off the clearing side, so even with the skeleton key in hand the player can't descend back through the grate. Add the down exit referencing the grate passage; traversableWhen on the passage already gates on isOpen, so the closed-grating message still applies symmetrically. Description override matches canonical Infocom prose.
rooms.description: You are in a small clearing in a well marked forest path that extends to the east and west.

rooms.$comment: living-room.down: ZIL declares the trap-door from cellar's side (cellar.up → living-room), but the descent direction from the living room itself is buried in routine logic the mechanical extractor skips. Add it explicitly so the player can actually descend through the open trap door. visibleWhen hides the exit until the rug is moved (so 'down' isn't even listed as a direction). Description: author rewrite — drop the gothic-lettering canon (felt out of place), name the kitchen behind the east doorway and the front door behind the west door (permanently nailed shut from west-of-house's side too).
rooms.description: You are in the living room. To the east, a doorway opens into the kitchen. To the west is the front door, nailed shut. A trophy case stands against one wall, and a large oriental rug lies in the center of the room.

rooms.$comment: white-cliffs-north: ZIL gates south + west on flag(deflate) — the canonical 'you're on foot, not in the raft' check for the unwired magic-boat puzzle. Since deflate is never set, both exits are permanently blocked, soft-locking the player who walks east from damp-cave (which has NO matching gate, so entry is one-way). Strip the gates so the beach is navigable on foot. When the boat puzzle eventually lands, re-add the gating with the proper boat-state condition.
white-cliffs-south: same deflate-flag soft-lock as north. Ungate so the player can walk back north along the beach.
sandy-beach still has the dig-with-shovel scarab dev note — that puzzle remains unwired even after the boat ships.
rooms.description:
You are on a large sandy beach on the east shore of the river, which is flowing quickly by. A path runs beside the river to the south here, and a passage is partially buried in sand to the northeast.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

This is a sand-filled cave whose exit is to the southwest.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible here without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

rooms.$comment: DARK ROOMS: canonical Zork I dark rooms get state.dark=true. Combined with the player having no perceivable lit light source, this satisfies the story-level darkness condition (defaultVisibility) and triggers the pitch-black sharedVariant + the grue tick triggers below.
Troll-room: dark + the troll blocks the east/west exits while alive. Override redeclares the gated exits so the blockedMessage attaches alongside the existing troll-flag gate.

east.blockedMessage: The troll's axe swings wildly — you cannot pass while he stands here, very much alive.

west.blockedMessage: The troll's axe swings wildly — you cannot pass while he stands here, very much alive.

rooms.$comment: Description override: append the canonical 'grating with skull-and-crossbones lock' sentence — Infocom's hint that the grate is the puzzle target. Lock is state-neutral (don't say 'locked' since the player can unlock it).
rooms.description: You are in a small room near the maze. There are twisty passages in the immediate vicinity. Above you is a grating with a skull-and-crossbones lock.

rooms.$comment: Description override: append the canonical 'loud roaring sound from below' hint — points the player toward the loud-room (echo puzzle).
rooms.description: You are on the south edge of a deep canyon. Passages lead off to the east, northwest and southwest. A stairway leads down. You can hear a loud roaring sound, like that of rushing water, from below.

rooms.$comment: Loud Room. Three states: default loud (water flowing over the dam top, base description); unbearably loud (gates open, water rushing through); eerily silent (echo OR drain+close). Variants are first-match — silent checked before unbearably-loud; default falls through to base.
rooms.description: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is deafeningly loud with an undetermined rushing sound. The sound seems to be reverberating from all of the walls, making it difficult even to think.

variants.$comment: Eerily silent — puzzle solved (echo-spoken OR drained-and-closed-gates).
variants.text: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is unnaturally quiet now, the rushing sound silenced as if it had never been.

variants.$comment: Unbearably loud — gates wide open, water rushing through the sluice. Even louder than the default 'over the top' state.
variants.text: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is unbearably loud — an ear-splitting torrent of water roars from somewhere unseen, drowning every other sense.

rooms.$comment: Torch-room override. Adds a permanently-blocked `up` exit so the LLM has canonical refusal text when the player tries to climb back up the rope (canonical Zork I: the rope ends some five feet above the player's head — visible from torch-room but unreachable). Descent is one-way; the player loops back via the coal mine + engravings-cave path. The dome-flag=true variant surfaces the rope hanging from above, with canonical 'ending some five feet above your head' wording so the out-of-reach status is textually explicit.

up.blockedMessage: The rope dangles overhead, but it ends some five feet above your head — too high to reach.

variants.text: This is a large room with a prominent doorway leading to a down staircase. Above you is a large dome, and a piece of rope descends from a wooden railing 20 feet up — the rope you tied, ending some five feet above your head. In the center of the room sits a white marble pedestal.

rooms.$comment: Entrance to Hades: restore canonical 'barred by evil spirits' text + variant for post-ritual state. Without this sentence (canonical Zork I per docs/zork-canonical-text.md:404), the room description claims 'gate is open' but the engine refuses traversal — the LLM has no textual reason for the refusal and improvises confused prose. Variant flips when the bell+candles+book ritual sets lld-flag. Exits re-declared with blockedMessages so the LLM has canonical refusal text if it tries to enter pre-ritual.
rooms.description: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| The gate is open; through it you can see a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. Thousands of voices, lamenting some hideous fate, can be heard. The way through the gate is barred by evil spirits, who jeer at your attempts to pass.

variants.$comment: Post-exorcism (lld-flag=true): the spirits have fled; the gate is now genuinely passable.
variants.text: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| The gate is open; through it you can see a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The wailing voices have fallen silent — the spirits that barred your passage have fled. The way is clear.

variants.$comment: Bell rang here AND candles currently lit. State description only.
variants.text: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| Beyond the gate, a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The bell lies on the ground where it fell, the red glow gone out of it. The candles burn with strange dancing flames. The spirits cower, trembling.

variants.$comment: Bell rang here, candles not currently lit. State description only.
variants.text: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| Beyond the gate, a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The bell lies on the ground where it fell, the red glow gone out of it. The spirits stand paralyzed, watching you with the expression of long-forgotten terror.

in.blockedMessage: The evil spirits jeer and bar your passage — you cannot enter the Land of the Dead until they have been driven off.

south.blockedMessage: The evil spirits jeer and bar your passage — you cannot enter the Land of the Dead until they have been driven off.

rooms.$comment: Description override: append the canonical bat sentence — Infocom's hint that the room contains the bat (the garlic puzzle hinges on knowing the bat is here).
rooms.description: You are in a small room which has doors only to the east and south. In the corner of the room on the ceiling is a large vampire bat who is obviously deranged and holding his nose.

rooms.$comment: timber-room.west is hidden when blocked so the LLM doesn't see the exit toggle between turns and dramatize it ('the way is now open / blocked again'). The room description still tells the player about the narrow passage to the west; the empty-handed gate stays the canonical mechanic.
lower-shaft return exits (out, east) hidden when blocked — same reasoning as timber-room.west.
Description: state-neutral base ends at 'a large lid'. Variants append the canonical lid state (open/closed) for each visit.
rooms.description: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid.

variants.text: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is open.

This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is closed.

rooms.$comment: Cyclops-room: the cyclops blocks east (his hunger / rage gate via magic-flag) and up (the staircase, gate via cyclops-flag — fled). Override adds blockedMessages for both.

east.blockedMessage: The cyclops, a hulking giant, blocks the passage east, glowering hungrily down at you.

up.blockedMessage: The cyclops watches the staircase with his single fierce eye — you'd never get past him alive.

rooms.$comment: West-of-house: the stone barrow path is sealed until the player has won the game (won-flag). Override redeclares the two barrow exits with thematic refusal text.

southwest.blockedMessage: The path to the barrow lies in shadow. It will not open to you yet.

in.blockedMessage: The path to the barrow lies in shadow. It will not open to you yet.

rooms.$comment: Description: state-neutral base; variants append the canonical window state (slightly ajar / open) so the LDESC reads canonically each visit.
rooms.description: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window.

variants.text: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is open.
You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.$comment: Description: state-neutral base. Variant adds the canonical 'A solid rainbow spans the falls.' once the player has waved the sceptre (rainbow-flag set) — that's the puzzle hint that the rainbow is now traversable.
rooms.description: You are at the top of Aragain Falls, an enormous waterfall with a drop of about 450 feet. The only path here is on the north end.

variants.text: You are at the top of Aragain Falls, an enormous waterfall with a drop of about 450 feet. The only path here is on the north end. A solid rainbow spans the falls.

items.$comment: Authored descriptions — strictly item-focused (no positional language). Voice: terse, concrete, faintly dry — Infocom house style. Describe what the THING looks/feels like, never where it sits.
items.description: Dense pine and hemlock, the canopy filtering daylight to a green twilight.
A large leaf-bearing tree, lower branches at climbing height.
An impassable wall of high peaks — the Flatheads, named (legend has it) for their first king.
Cold dark water moving with quiet, steady force. The current is swift; rocks lie half-hidden below.
Sheer chalk-white cliffs, scoured smooth and quite unscalable.
A rough cliff face, weathered into ledges and handholds. Climbing it is possible — and perilous.

items.$comment: Rainbow scenery. Suppress the LLM's tendency to editorialize about walkability — canonical Zork describes the rainbow purely visually until the player solidifies it with the sceptre. Adding 'you cannot walk upon it' (or its inverse) is engine-side state inference the LLM shouldn't surface. Engine exits gate on rainbow-flag and reject traversal if not set; that's the only authority on whether you can step onto it.
items.description: A perfect arc of color, shimmering with a strange and almost solid presence.
items.narratorNote: Describe the rainbow strictly as a visual phenomenon — colors arcing over the falls. Never volunteer claims about its walkability or solidity in either direction (do NOT say 'you cannot walk on it' or 'it looks solid enough to walk on'). The engine controls traversal via the rainbow-flag-gated exits; if the player attempts to walk onto the rainbow, the engine will either accept or reject and you narrate the actual outcome — but never preemptively comment on its walkability in the room or item description.

items.description: Fine pale sand, soft underfoot — the sort that responds well to a shovel.
A narrow vertical crack — far too thin for anyone to slip through.
Cold dark water — drinkable, you suppose, though the thought isn't appetizing.
A measure of clear water, transparent and quite ordinary.
Translucent figures drifting through the air, faces blank with ancient grief. They take no notice of you.
A heap of corpses, far too long-dead to identify. Disturbing them feels like a very poor idea.

items.$comment: Bat is ACTORBIT in canonical Zork ZIL — it triggers the sword's I-SWORD glow daemon. Mark hostile=true so anyPerceivableItemWith / anyAdjacentRoomItemWith pick it up.
items.description: A sleek vampire bat, watching with bright unblinking eyes — and a mouth full of small sharp teeth.
An inscription in an ancient script — a prayer of safe passage, you somehow understand.
A window securely boarded over from within. There's no entering this way.
A great mound of weathered stone, ancient beyond reckoning, with a single massive door.
An enormous stone door, impossibly heavy. It would take more than human strength to budge.
A narrow flight of stone steps ascending into shadow.
A sturdy wooden ladder, rungs worn smooth by use.
A worn wooden railing, polished smooth by countless hands.

items.$comment: Dam water-flow indicator. Three variants over the (gates-open, low-tide) state pair. First-match: torrent-through-gates checked first, then drained-and-closed silent window, then drained-and-still-open trickle. Default base is the initial cascading-over-the-top state.
items.appearance: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure looming above the river.
items.description: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure of evident bureaucratic origin. Water cascades over its top in a steady, thunderous fall.

variants.$comment: Gates wide open, reservoir not yet drained — water roars through.
variants.text: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure. The sluice gates are wide open; water roars through in a savage torrent. The reservoir level is visibly falling.

variants.$comment: Silent window — gates closed AND reservoir drained.
variants.text: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure. The reservoir behind it is drained to a mud-flat; with the sluice gates closed and the water gone, the dam stands eerily still.

variants.$comment: Gates re-opened after drain — only a trickle remains.
variants.text: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure. The sluice gates are wide open and the reservoir is drained; only a thin trickle still passes through.

items.description: A bank of indicator lights, four colored buttons, and a single iron bolt — clearly some kind of maintenance station.

items.$comment: Dam control bolt. Requires the wrench to turn — but the player must EXPLICITLY say which tool. Never auto-fill the wrench from inventory; if the player says 'turn bolt' alone, ask them what to use.
items.appearance: A heavy iron bolt protrudes from the dam's control panel.
items.description: A heavy iron bolt with a slot evidently sized for a wrench.
items.narratorNote: DEFAULT to turn-dam-bolt-by-hand (no args) for ANY input about the bolt unless the player's input literally names a tool. Inputs like 'turn the bolt', 'twist bolt', 'open the gates', 'open the dam' → turn-dam-bolt-by-hand. ONLY call turn-dam-bolt when the player's input contains a literal tool noun like 'wrench' (e.g. 'turn bolt with wrench', 'use the wrench on the bolt'). NEVER auto-fill withItemId from inventory. This applies to both opening and closing the gates.

items.$comment: Dam-panel indicator. Glows when gate-flag=true (yellow button pressed) — the bolt control is live. yellow-button-enables-bolt narrates the flicker once; this variant makes the state persistently readable on examine bubble.
items.appearance: A small green glass bubble is set into the dam's control panel.
items.description: A small green glass bubble — currently dark, evidently waiting for something.

variants.text: A small green glass bubble, glowing steadily — the bolt control is live.

items.appearance: A worn yellow button is set into the panel.
items.description: A worn yellow button, slightly recessed. Yellow paint flakes around the edges.

items.appearance: A small brown button sits beside the yellow.
items.description: A small brown button, smooth and unmarked.

items.appearance: A red button with a faded warning symbol sits on the panel.
items.description: A red button marked with a faded warning symbol.

items.appearance: A small blue button is set into the panel.
items.description: A small blue button, smooth and unmarked.
Water trickles from a small leak — for now a nuisance rather than a disaster.
A complicated piece of machinery: gears, curious nozzles, a hinged lid, and a small switch on one flank.
A small switch with a slot evidently sized for a screwdriver.
items.narratorNote: DEFAULT to push-machine-switch (no args) for ANY input about the START switch unless the player's input literally names a tool. Inputs like 'push start', 'start the machine', 'press switch', 'turn it on', 'activate', 'operate switch' → push-machine-switch. ONLY call turn-machine-switch when the player's input contains a literal tool noun like 'screwdriver' (e.g. 'turn switch with screwdriver', 'use the screwdriver on the switch'). NEVER auto-fill withItemId from inventory.

items.description: A long metal chute angling steeply downward into darkness. Once you commit, climbing back out looks impossible.
A length of broken timber, splintered at one end and surprisingly heavy.

items.$comment: Brass bell. state.rangAtHades is the persistent fact the LLD ritual gates on — set when bell is rung at entrance-to-hades. Bell heat is purely narrative (no isHot state); the bell-rings trigger's narration describes it becoming red-hot and dropping, but by next turn the player can pick it up freely.
items.description: A small brass bell with a worn handle, polished smooth by use.
A weathered skeleton, long picked clean. A small leather bag lies near one outstretched hand.
A pungent clove of garlic, its papery skin crackling at the slightest touch.
A small hand-held pump — the sort you'd use for a bicycle tire, or perhaps a folded raft.
A lump of viscous greyish material, evidently good for sealing a puncture or two.
A standard flat-head screwdriver, well-worn but serviceable.
A long-handled shovel, well-balanced for digging.
A solid iron wrench, sized for a familiar bolt.
A small heap of dusty black coal — surprisingly heavy, and very dirty.
A flawless emerald the size of a small egg, its green depths catching every gleam of light. A treasure.
An exquisite scarab beetle carved from a single piece of jade and chased with gold. A treasure.
A delicate bracelet set with sapphires, glittering even in dim light. A treasure.

items.$comment: Single physical basket suspended on a chain in the shaft. Initial position: raised at shaft-room. Lowered/raised by intent → basket-lowers / basket-raises triggers move the item between shaft-room and lower-shaft and toggle position state. Items put into the basket follow it transitively. Wicker construction = always open; openWhen/closeWhen suppressed. The chain spans both ends of the shaft, so the basket appearsIn lower-shaft as well as its current location — appearanceVariants give room-and-position-aware prose so the LLM doesn't hallucinate a basket from the room's chain mention. Default appearance handles the default case (player at shaft-room, basket raised).
items.name: large basket
items.description: A large wicker basket suspended from a chain that disappears upward into the darkness of the shaft.
items.appearance: A large wicker basket hangs from a heavy iron chain, suspended over the mouth of the shaft.

appearanceVariants.text: Far above you at the top of the shaft, the wicker basket sways gently on its chain.
A large wicker basket rests at your feet, the chain rising into the shaft above.
The chain dangles down into the shaft. The basket has been lowered out of sight below.

items.narratorNote: The basket is a freight elevator on a chain. It can only be moved by 'lower basket' or 'raise basket' (use the lower-basket / raise-basket intents). It cannot be picked up or carried; nor can it be opened or closed (it has no lid). Treat it as a fixed feature whose only state is up vs. down. The chain spans both ends of the shaft, so the basket is perceivable from BOTH shaft-room and lower-shaft, but with different prose: from above you see it (or its absence), from below you see it overhead (or at your feet).

items.$comment: Book is a sacred-text container with an open/close mechanic — but you can NEVER put anything in it (the container.accessibleWhen is overridden to always-false, regardless of isOpen). state.isOpen is preserved so open/close work for narrative flavor. Description and appearance both have variants for the open and closed states so the LLM has clean text to narrate either way. read(book) returns the prayer text directly (engine doesn't gate read on container). put(X, book) is rejected with the snarky accessBlockedMessage. Starts closed.
items.appearance: A large black book lies here, its cover closed.

appearanceVariants.text: A large black book lies here, held open to page 569.

items.description: A large black book bound in cracked leather. The cover is closed and weighty; the words within are not for the curious.

variants.text: A large black book bound in cracked leather, held open to page 569. The text is dense, archaic — a ritual prayer of some kind.

accessibleWhen.$comment: Always false. Override fully replaces the auto-gen accessibleWhen (which gated on isOpen=true) — book is sacred-text, never has anything put into it. The null entries strip the inherited itemId/key/equals fields so the merged Condition is a clean { type: 'not', condition: { type: 'always' } }.

container.accessBlockedMessage: You'd really stuff something into a sacred ritual text? The pages are not for that.

items.description: An elongated brown paper sack, smelling powerfully of hot peppers.
A silver chalice, intricately engraved with looping vines. A treasure.
Poseidon's own trident, carved from a single piece of crystal. A treasure.
The solid-gold coffin used for the burial of Ramses II — heavy, ornate, and worth a king's ransom. A treasure.
An exquisite jade figurine, carved with delicate features. A treasure.
A battered brass lantern, its battery long since drained dry.

items.$comment: Matchbook with 5 matches and a burn-state. The generic light(itemId=match) tool fires the match-strikes trigger, which consumes a match and starts a 2-turn countdown (match-burn-countdown flag). The candles-light trigger requires match.isLit==true (must use a burning match) and clears it. afterAction triggers tick the countdown and auto-extinguish on timeout. State key `isLit` is harmonized with lamp/candles/torch so the generic light/extinguish tools work uniformly.
items.description: A matchbook whose cover advertises FCD#3 in chipper bureaucratic prose.

items.$comment: Candles burn-time mirrors the lamp-battery model. Canonical Zork I: candles burn for ~230 turns once lit, then go out permanently (FLAMEBIT items in ZIL had finite fuses). state.isLit is set during extraction (candles start lit at south-temple altar). burnTurnsRemaining ticks down each turn while lit; when it hits 0 the candles-burn-out trigger force-extinguishes them. The candles-light-spent soft-fail blocks re-lighting once the wax is gone.
items.description: A pair of long burning candles, dripping wax steadily.
A pile of dry leaves, gathered as if by wind.
A red metal buoy, weathered by long exposure — evidently a warning.

items.$comment: Reservoir treasure. Half-buried in the mud at the bottom of the reservoir — only visible at low-tide (when the reservoir is drained).
items.description: An old wooden trunk, mud-caked and bulging with jewels. A treasure.
A small metal tube of the sort that holds toothpaste — but its contents are something else entirely.
Old engravings worked deep into stone — ancient writing, half worn away.
A small bird's nest, woven of twigs and grass.
A golden clockwork canary with ruby eyes and a silver beak. A crystal window below its left wing reveals intricate machinery; the mechanism appears to have wound down. A treasure.
An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.
An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.

items.$comment: SURFACE: kitchen-table is canonically a flat surface (CONTBIT in ZIL so the parser handles 'put X on table', no OPENBIT). state.isOpen + accessibleWhen are nulled out so the close handler refuses ('isn't something you can close'). Real description + narratorNote tell the LLM how to handle the on-vs-in mismatch.
items.description: A long wooden kitchen table, scarred by years of food preparation.
items.narratorNote: This is a flat work surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the kitchen-table (sandwich-bag, bottle) are actually resting ON its surface — narrate them as such. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: attic-table — same shape as kitchen-table.
items.description: A heavy old wooden table, varnish worn through in patches.
items.narratorNote: Flat surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' this table are resting ON its surface. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: altar in south-temple. A flat stone surface for offerings.
items.description: A massive stone altar, carved with worn ritual figures and stained dark by centuries of offerings.
items.narratorNote: The altar is a flat ritual surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the altar (candles, the black book) are actually resting ON top of it. Never refer to the altar as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: pedestal in torch-room. A stone column with a torch on top.
items.description: A polished stone pedestal, waist-high.
items.narratorNote: A short column, not a container. Items located 'in' the pedestal (the torch) are resting ON its top. Never refer to the pedestal as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: Egg puzzle: the clasp is too delicate for direct opening. Suppress the auto-gen 'open egg' intent (openWhen: never) and let the give-egg-to-songbird intent + trigger handle opening. Force-opening (smashing) destroys the canary inside — that's a future trigger.
items.narratorNote: The egg, once opened, cannot be closed again. The clasp is delicate filigree; once sprung, it bends out of true and refuses to seat. If the player tries to close it, narrate the failure in flavor — the clasp is a one-shot mechanism, the gold inlay has buckled, the tiny hinge-pin was lost into the songbird's beak (or thief's pocket), etc. Pick a silly-but-period-appropriate reason; never say 'the engine doesn't allow it.'

items.$comment: Brass lantern with battery. state.isLit governs whether it emits light (per the perceivable-light convention used by defaultVisibility). state.batteryTurns counts down each turn while lit; at 0 the lamp auto-extinguishes (lamp-runs-out tick trigger). Canonical Zork battery is ~330 turns.

Troll for combat. fromTemplate gives him the shared troll personality + status state (unconscious/knocked-down/disarmed/hostile baseline) plus the enemy/troll/humanoid tags. Per-instance fields below: state.health (10) and the description.
items.appearance: A nasty-looking troll, hulking and brutish, brandishes a bloody axe.
items.personality: A nasty, hulking creature with greasy black hair and broken yellow tusks. Speaks in broken Common — short sentences, aggressive, third person ('Troll smash!'). Hates elves and dwarves; covets shiny things. If addressed in a friendly tone, suspicious. If threatened, roars. If knocked unconscious, snores and mutters about gold. Will not converse meaningfully — short menacing lines only.

items.$comment: Elvish sword. fromTemplate('bladed-weapon') gives it the weapon/bladed/carryable tags + base weapon state (weapon-stuck, attack-bonus-*). Tags 'sword' and 'magical' are unioned in. damage:5 layered on per-instance. Two glow tiers per canonical Zork I I-SWORD daemon: bright when a hostile is in the same room (anyPerceivableItemWith), faint when a hostile is in any directly-adjacent room (anyAdjacentRoomItemWith — walks exits regardless of door state, like ZIL UEXIT/CEXIT/DEXIT). Variants are first-match, so the bright tier wins when both would trigger.
items.appearance: An elvish sword of great antiquity, blade etched with fine runes.
items.description: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes.

variants.text: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes — and presently glowing very brightly.
An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes — and presently glowing with a faint blue light.

items.$comment: Rusty knife — feeble bladed weapon. fromTemplate provides shared bladed-weapon shape; tag 'knife' unioned in; damage 2.
items.description: A rusty knife, the blade pitted but still serviceable.

items.$comment: Troll's axe. After the troll drops it (disarm outcome OR death), the player can pick it up and either swing it or throw it — different mode triggers, different outcome tables. fromTemplate gives shared bladed-weapon state; tags 'axe' and 'throwable' unioned in.
items.description: A heavy iron axe, its head crusted with dried blood.

items.$comment: leaflet item id is 'advertisement' in the ZIL extraction (the mailbox leaflet that explains the game).
items.description: A small printed leaflet, the paper a little crumpled.

items.$comment: Nasty knife — the thief's weapon. Tag it as bladed so the class trigger covers it too if the player ever wields it.
items.description: A nasty-looking knife, the blade dull but evidently well-used.
A hot pepper sandwich wrapped tightly in waxed paper. Smells fierce.
A clear glass bottle, sealed at the neck.

items.appearance: A small dented mailbox stands here on its post.
items.description: A small mailbox of dented metal, fixed firmly to a post outside the front door.

items.appearance: A handsome glass-fronted trophy case stands against one wall.
items.description: A handsome glass-fronted display case — clearly meant to hold a collector's prizes.

items.personality: A bright forest songbird with a clear, perfect trill. Doesn't speak — communicates through song. Curious about shiny things, especially the jewel-encrusted egg. Gentle, never threatening.
items.description: A small bright-feathered songbird, watching you with curious black eyes. Its trill is impossibly pure.

items.$comment: Cyclops gets HP, hunger counter, and combat status state. hunger ticks up while the player lingers in his room; at threshold he attacks. Health is high (huge enemy); class triggers will favor misses over kills. Description matches canonical Infocom — note the 'though he likes people' clue, the canonical hint that points the player toward the ULYSSES solution.
items.appearance: A massive cyclops looms in the room, a single hungry eye fixed on you.
items.description: A cyclops, who looks prepared to eat horses (much less mere adventurers), blocks the staircase. From his state of health, and the bloodstains on the walls, you gather that he is not very friendly, though he likes people.
items.personality: A massive, single-eyed brute, hungry and angry. Speaks in slow rumbling Common — full sentences but simple words. Demands food and drink. Terrified of the name of the Greek hero who blinded his ancestor. If fed, becomes drowsy and friendly; if threatened, roars and lunges.

items.$comment: Thief gets HP, autonomy state. appeared:false until the global turn counter reaches the threshold (30 turns). openWhen+closeWhen suppress the auto-gen open/close intents that fire because the extractor saw the thief as a CONTBIT (he carries stolen things). Players opening the thief is silly; the player attacking him for his loot is the path.
items.appearance: A suspicious-looking gentleman in a black cloak watches you, fingering a stiletto.
items.personality: A suave, mustachioed gentleman thief in a black cloak. Speaks in clipped, ironic phrases ('How droll.', 'A trifle, surely?'). Will steal valuables on sight; pockets things and vanishes. Treats every encounter as an aesthetic event — never crude, always theatrical.
items.description: A suspicious-looking gentleman in a black cloak, holding a large bag and armed with a deadly stiletto.

items.$comment: Rope-and-dome puzzle item. Default (loose / in inventory / in attic): plain rope. Tied state (rope at dome-room with dome-flag=true): appearance + description variants describe the tied configuration AND make the take-as-untie mechanic explicit, so the LLM doesn't refuse a take with hallucinated 'must untie first' logic. narratorNote is engine-side guidance; the LLM follows silently and never quotes it.
items.appearance: A long coil of rope lies here.

appearanceVariants.text: A rope is tied securely to the wooden railing here, descending into the darkness below.

items.description: A long coil of strong rope.

variants.text: A long coil of strong rope, currently tied to the wooden railing and descending into the darkness below. Taking the rope is the way to untie it.

items.narratorNote: When the rope is at dome-room with dome-flag=true (tied), take(rope) is the canonical untie mechanic — the engine fires the untie-rope-on-take trigger, moves the rope into the player's inventory, and clears dome-flag. NEVER refuse a take with 'you must untie first' — taking IS untying. For player intents like 'untie rope', 'get the rope back', 'retrieve rope', or 'untie it' while the rope is tied, always call take(rope).

items.description: A heavy oriental carpet covers the center of the floor, oddly out of place. Something about its position invites curiosity.
A skeleton key of dark iron, deeply worn — clearly very old.

items.$comment: Torch state.isLit defaults to true — canonical Zork: the torch is perpetually lit (it's wreathed in flame from the moment the player first sees it). The generic extinguish tool would normally flip it to false; the torch-extinguish-noop trigger reverses that and narrates a refusal. Existing puzzle triggers (gas-room-explodes, burn-leaves) check torch.isLit, which now resolves correctly from the start.
items.description: A flaming torch, the haft carved from ivory and the head wreathed in clean flame. A treasure.

items.$comment: Maze treasure. Listed in win condition; tagging it 'treasure' lets future score-counter triggers walk all treasure-tagged items rather than enumerate by id.
items.description: An old leather bag, bulging with coins. A treasure.

items.$comment: Land of the Living Dead treasure. The crystal skull of an ancient adventurer.
items.description: A beautifully carved crystal skull, grinning at you rather nastily. A treasure.

items.$comment: Echo-room treasure. Hidden in canonical Zork until the loud-room is silent. Two silencing paths: (a) say 'echo' (easter egg, sets echo-spoken=true), or (b) drain reservoir via dam controls and close gates (low-tide AND NOT gates-open). Visible in EITHER state.
items.description: A heavy platinum bar, dense and gleaming dully. A treasure.

items.$comment: Rainbow treasure. INVISIBLE in canonical Zork until the player waves the sceptre at end-of-rainbow. We adapt via visibleWhen on rainbow-flag.
items.description: A heavy iron pot brimming with gold coins. A treasure.

items.$comment: NEW item appended via mergeItems — diamond produced by the coal-machine puzzle. Starts at 'nowhere'; the machine-trigger moves it to the machine on success.
items.name: huge diamond
items.description: A magnificent flawless diamond, gleaming with inner fire.

items.$comment: NEW item appended via mergeItems — brass bauble delivered by the songbird when the canary is wound in a forest room. Starts at 'nowhere'; the canary-sings-for-bauble trigger moves it to the player's room (or to 'path' if winding from up-a-tree).
items.name: beautiful brass bauble
items.description: A small, glittering brass bauble — surprisingly heavy for its size. A treasure.

items.$comment: Tag scepter as a weapon so the inventoryHasTag(weapon) check on the boat catches it. Canonical Zork includes SCEPTRE in the boat-puncture list because it has WEAPONBIT — the magic boat treats it as sharp. Description rewritten state-neutrally (canonical 'is in the coffin' is positional and contradicts the description once taken). 'Every color of the rainbow' is the puzzle hint pointing to the wave-sceptre-at-rainbow solution.
items.description: An ancient Egyptian sceptre, ornamented with bands of enamel in every color of the rainbow. The shaft tapers to a sharp point.

items.$comment: Tag thief's stiletto as a weapon (canonical Zork has it in WEAPONBIT). Used for the boat-puncture check.
items.description: A slim, wickedly pointed stiletto — the thief's signature weapon.

items.$comment: Make the inflatable boat a real engine vehicle. state.inflation: 'deflated' (default) | 'inflated' | 'punctured'. The vehicle.enterableWhen gate ensures players can only board the inflated form. mobile=true so the boat travels with the player on go() — drifts downstream with the current.

vehicle.enterBlockedMessage: The boat is a flat pile of plastic — it'd need to be inflated first.

items.description: A folded square of yellow plastic with a small valve attached — clearly intended to be inflated.

$customToolsComment: Custom tools are author-defined LLM tools. Each becomes a tool the narrator can call directly with typed args. Handlers do the generic state mutation (open sets isOpen=true on any item with that state key); per-puzzle special cases are layered on as triggers that match on intentMatched + intentArg. open/close/break here replace the dozens of per-item intents that the auto-gen used to emit.

customTools.$comment: SCORE verb. Player asks for current standing; engine emits the canonical Zork phrasing with live values via narration template tokens.
customTools.description: Player asks about their current score, rank, moves, or progress (e.g. 'score', 'how am I doing?', 'what's my score?'). Always available.

handler.successNarration: Your score is {flag.score} (total of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This gives you the rank of {rank}.

customTools.$comment: Open handler — works for both items (mailbox, trophy case) AND passages (kitchen window, trap door). The arg is named itemId for backward compat, but the value can be either an item id or a passage id from the view. Each precondition is an OR over item+passage variants; effects emit BOTH setItemState and setPassageState — the one matching the id mutates, the other defensively no-ops.
customTools.description: Player opens, pulls open, lifts the lid of, unlatches, or otherwise opens an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.). Pass the id from the current view's itemsHere or passagesHere. The engine's handler reports failure when the target isn't accessible, isn't openable, or is already open — narrate from those cues.

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to open (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.$comment: Close handler — symmetric to open. Same item-or-passage polymorphism.
customTools.description: Player closes, shuts, latches, lids, or otherwise closes an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.).

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to close (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.description: Player breaks, smashes, shatters, hurls, or otherwise destroys an item. Once broken, an item stays broken — opening or repairing it requires a separate puzzle.

preconditions.failedNarration: {arg.itemId.name} doesn't seem like something you could break.

customTools.$comment: Rope-and-dome puzzle. Player ties the rope to the railing in the Dome Room; that flips dome-flag, unlocking the existing dome-room.down → torch-room exit. Active only when you're standing in the dome with the rope (and haven't already done it — once-matched intents auto-deactivate).
customTools.description: Player ties, fastens, attaches, lashes, secures, or otherwise affixes the rope to the railing (or to the dome).

customTools.$comment: Egg puzzle: give the jewel-encrusted egg to the songbird; her song springs the clasp open and reveals the canary. Songbird is in forest-1 with appearsIn covering forest-2/3, path, up-a-tree, clearing — gate on player being in any of those. Inactive after egg is open.
customTools.description: Player gives the jewel-encrusted egg to the songbird, offers it to the bird, hands it to her, presents it to the singer.

customTools.$comment: Alternate egg-opening path. Give the closed jewel-encrusted egg to the thief; he opens it cleanly with a flick and pockets both the egg and the canary. Recovered when the thief dies. Canon: this is the second of two ways to open the egg without breaking it (the first is give-egg-to-songbird).
customTools.description: Player gives, offers, hands, or presents the jewel-encrusted egg to the thief.

customTools.$comment: Move-the-rug puzzle. Living-room rug hides the trap door. Once moved, rug-moved-flag flips and the trap-door's openWhen passes. Active only when in living-room and not yet matched.
customTools.description: Player moves, slides, lifts, pulls aside, kicks aside, rolls up, or otherwise displaces the rug (carpet) in the living room.

customTools.$comment: Cyclops magic word. Saying 'ulysses' or 'odysseus' (referencing the Greek hero who blinded the cyclops Polyphemus) terrifies him. He flees east, smashing through the wall to make a new passage to the living room. Sets both cyclops-flag (he's gone, can climb up to treasure room) and magic-flag (the wall is broken, opens cyclops-room↔living-room shortcut).
customTools.description: Player says, shouts, whispers, or invokes 'Ulysses', 'Odysseus', or otherwise utters the magic word that terrifies the cyclops.

customTools.$comment: Cyclops feed alternative. Give him the hot pepper sandwich (lunch) and water bottle. He eats, drinks, and falls asleep. Sets cyclops-flag (no longer blocking) but NOT magic-flag (no wall smash — slept, didn't flee). Different aftermath than the magic word.
customTools.description: Player gives, offers, or hands the cyclops the lunch (hot pepper sandwich) and the bottle of water — feeding and watering him.

customTools.$comment: Pray at the altar in south-temple. Teleports the player to forest-1 (canonical Zork: prayer at the altar transports the adventurer back to the surface).
customTools.description: Player prays, kneels, makes an offering, or performs a ritual at the altar.

customTools.$comment: Light any lightable item — lantern, candles, match, torch, etc. Generic handler: gates on accessibility + presence of state.isLit. Sets isLit=true unconditionally; per-item triggers (lamp-lights, match-strikes, candles-light) gate on intentArg(light, itemId, X) + per-item conditions and emit the canonical narration. We deliberately skip an `isLit==false` precondition so the handler's failedNarration can't double-up with the trigger's success narration on duplicate calls — a duplicate `light X` simply re-fires the trigger's prose, which the LLM handles cleanly.
customTools.description: Player lights, ignites, kindles, switches on, fires up, or activates a light-bearing item (lantern, candles, match, torch). Pass the id of an item from the current view's inventory or itemsHere.

itemId.description: id of the item to light

customTools.$comment: Extinguish any lit item. Mirrors `light`: gates on accessibility + isLit state-key. Sets isLit=false; per-item triggers handle side effects + canonical narration. Same reason as `light` for skipping the isLit==true precondition.
customTools.description: Player extinguishes, douses, blows out, snuffs, switches off, or puts out a lit item (lantern, candles, match, torch). Pass the id of an item from the current view's inventory or itemsHere.

itemId.description: id of the item to extinguish

customTools.$comment: Player tries to free a stuck weapon (sword/axe/knife). Active whenever any of the bladed weapons is in the weapon-stuck state. The trigger picks the right weapon by checking each individually.
customTools.description: Player tries to free, yank, pull, dislodge, retrieve, or wrench loose their stuck weapon (sword, knife, or axe).

customTools.$comment: TALK-TO-TROLL: player engages the troll conversationally. Each match bumps troll.state.aggravation; at threshold the troll snaps and starts attacking (NPC-initiated combat). Active only while the troll is alive, present, and not yet engaged. The narrator records this BEFORE narrating the troll's response in character.
customTools.description: Player tries to talk to, address, ask, shout at, insult, threaten, bargain with, beg, taunt, or otherwise engage the troll conversationally.

customTools.$comment: TALK-TO-CYCLOPS: same shape as talk-to-troll. Bumps cyclops hunger by an extra +1 per talk (over the passive hunger tick), so an aggressive talker provokes him faster. Active while cyclops alive, present, not yet appeased.
customTools.description: Player tries to talk to, address, ask, taunt, insult, threaten, or otherwise engage the cyclops conversationally — but NOT saying 'Ulysses' or 'Odysseus' (those match cyclops-magic-word instead).

customTools.$comment: ECHO room — say 'echo' in loud-room. Bare verb in canonical Zork (special command loop in LOUD-ROOM-FCN). We accept any phrasing that's clearly invoking the echo: 'echo', 'say echo', 'shout echo', 'call out echo'.
customTools.description: Player says, shouts, calls, invokes, or otherwise utters the word 'echo' in the loud room.

customTools.$comment: WAVE SCEPTRE at the rainbow. Active at end-of-rainbow OR aragain-falls (per canonical SCEPTRE-FUNCTION). Requires the sceptre in inventory. Wave / raise / brandish / point all count.
customTools.description: Player waves, raises, brandishes, points, or otherwise invokes the sceptre at the rainbow.
Player winds, twists, turns the key on, cranks, or activates the clockwork canary.
Player brushes, polishes, scrubs, or cleans their teeth using the rubbery putty/glue/gunk.
Player sets fire to, ignites, or burns the pile of leaves (with match, candles, torch, or any flame).
Player sets fire to, ignites, or burns the black book (the ritual book of arcane lore).
Player leaps, jumps, dives, or hurls themselves down/off the current location (a tree, a canyon edge, a cliff).
Player desecrates, mutilates, destroys, or otherwise interferes with the pile of corpses at the entrance to Hades.

customTools.$comment: Always available. The throw-at-self-dies trigger has no location/state gate (the player can hurl any inventory item at themselves anywhere) — making this customTool always-on moves it to the cache-stable tier instead of being re-injected as a per-turn dynamic tool.
customTools.description: Player throws, hurls, or chucks an object directly at themselves.

customTools.$comment: Always available. Same shape as throw-at-self — suicide-attack-self trigger only requires inventoryHasTag('weapon' OR 'bladed'); it can fire anywhere the player has any weapon. alwaysAvailable so it lives in the cache-stable tool tier.
customTools.description: Player attacks, slashes, stabs, or otherwise turns a weapon on themselves.
Player digs in the sand on the beach (with the shovel or by hand).

customTools.$comment: CLEAR-RUBBLE — the obvious thing to try when the timber-room blocks the way west. We don't actually clear anything (the canonical mechanic is empty-handed squeeze, not debris removal). Handler narrates a thematic refusal that reiterates the tight passage. Outside the timber-room, the action no-ops with a generic 'nothing to clear' line.
customTools.description: Player tries to clear, move, push, shove, drag, remove, dig out, or otherwise dislodge the broken timbers / rubble / debris that clutters the timber-room (or any similar passage they think is blocked by debris).

preconditions.failedNarration: There's nothing here you could call rubble.

handler.successNarration: You strain at the splintered timbers, but they're heavy and tightly wedged. Past them the passage narrows to a crack barely a person's width — clearing the rubble wouldn't widen it.

customTools.description: Player mutilates, defaces, slashes, or otherwise destroys the painting.
Player breaks, snaps, smashes, or otherwise destroys the sceptre (especially while at the rainbow).
Player jumps into, swims in, dives into, or wades through the Frigid River — without using the boat.

customTools.$comment: RUB the mirror in either mirror-room. Canonical mechanic teleports the player to the OTHER mirror room. We accept rub / touch / press / lean against / step into.
customTools.description: Player rubs, touches, presses, leans against, steps into, or otherwise interacts physically with the mirror.

customTools.$comment: RING the bell. alwaysAvailable for cache stability. The consuming trigger gates on hasItem(bell) AND not-yet-rung; if the player isn't carrying the bell, the trigger silently no-ops.
customTools.description: Player rings, tolls, peals, sounds, or otherwise activates the bell.

customTools.$comment: Read an item with readable text. Generic handler: gates on accessibility + readability, surfaces the item's readable.text via the {arg.itemId.readable.text} template. Side effects (puzzle triggers like LLD ritual completion) are wired via separate triggers gated on intentMatched(read) + intentArg(read, itemId, ...).
customTools.description: Read the contents of an item with readable text — book pages, leaflet, prayer inscription, matchbook cover, etc. Pass the id of an item from the current view's itemsHere or inventory.

itemId.description: id of the item to read

customTools.$comment: READ OTHER PAGES of the black book — canonical hint mechanism (per docs/puzzles.txt:51). The book's marked page (569) holds the prayer; other pages mention 'certain noises, lights, and prayers' are effective in banishing evil. Player can flip through anywhere they have the book.
customTools.description: Player flips through, browses, skims, or reads pages other than the dog-eared marked page (569) of the black book. NOT for reading the prayer itself (that's the regular `read` action on the book).

preconditions.failedNarration: You don't have the black book to flip through.

handler.successNarration: You leaf through the heavy black book, past the dog-eared page 569 to other passages. Among the dense archaic script, one line stands out: "In the banishing of evil, certain noises, lights, and prayers prove most effective."

customTools.$comment: TURN the machine switch WITH a named tool. Narrow tool: only fires when the player's input literally names the tool. DEFAULT for ALL other phrasings is push-machine-switch (the bare-hand pair). Broad operating verbs ('start', 'activate', 'operate', 'turn it on') belong on push-machine-switch — keep them OFF this description so the LLM doesn't match them here.
customTools.description: ONLY call when the player's input contains a literal tool name (e.g. 'turn switch WITH screwdriver', 'use the screwdriver on the switch', 'apply screwdriver to switch'). If the input does NOT contain a literal tool name, DO NOT call this tool — call push-machine-switch instead. Inputs like 'start the machine', 'press start', 'turn the switch', 'activate it', 'flip switch', 'operate switch', 'turn it on' do NOT name a tool — route those to push-machine-switch. NEVER auto-fill withItemId from inventory or context.

withItemId.description: the id of the tool the player literally named in their input (must come from the player's stated words — do not assume from inventory or context)

customTools.$comment: DEFAULT machine-switch tool. Carries the broad verb namespace ('start', 'activate', 'operate', 'push', 'press', 'flip', 'turn') so ambiguous inputs route here, not to turn-machine-switch. Trigger machine-switch-bare-hands emits the canonical refusal.
customTools.description: DEFAULT tool for the machine's START switch when the player has NOT named a tool. Player tries to start, push, press, poke, flip, tap, turn, activate, operate, run, trigger, engage, use, or turn-on the START switch / machine — any phrasing that doesn't literally name a tool. Examples: 'push start', 'start the machine', 'press the switch', 'flip the switch', 'turn the switch', 'activate the switch', 'operate the machine', 'use the switch', 'turn it on'. If the player names a tool (screwdriver, etc.), use turn-machine-switch instead.

customTools.$comment: LOWER the basket on its chain — basket descends from shaft-room to lower-shaft, taking any contents with it (transitive parentage). Operable from either room: pulling the chain from above lowers it; pulling the chain from below also lowers an already-raised basket toward you. Backed by the basket-lowers trigger.
customTools.description: Player lowers, drops, sends down, releases, pays out, or chains down the basket / dumbwaiter / chain into the shaft.

customTools.$comment: RAISE the basket on its chain — basket ascends from lower-shaft to shaft-room, taking any contents with it. Operable from either room. Backed by the basket-raises trigger.
customTools.description: Player raises, lifts, hauls up, draws up, pulls up, or winches up the basket / dumbwaiter / chain from the shaft.

customTools.$comment: PUSH the YELLOW button — enables the bolt control. Active in maintenance-room. Bare press is canonical.
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the yellow button.

customTools.$comment: PUSH the BROWN button — disables the bolt control. Active in maintenance-room.
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the brown button.

customTools.$comment: PUSH the RED button — toggles maintenance-room lights (cosmetic only; canonical Zork doesn't use this for any puzzle).
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the red button.

customTools.$comment: PUSH the BLUE button — triggers a leak in the maintenance-room ceiling pipe. Canonical: water rises over several turns; player must plug the leak with the viscous material (putty) before drowning.
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the blue button.

customTools.$comment: PLUG the leak WITH a named material. Narrow tool: only fires when the player's input literally names the material. DEFAULT for ALL other phrasings is plug-leak-by-hand. Broad verbs ('plug', 'stop', 'seal', 'fix') belong on plug-leak-by-hand — keep them OFF this description.
customTools.description: ONLY call when the player's input contains a literal material name (e.g. 'plug leak WITH putty', 'use the putty on the leak', 'apply putty'). If the input does NOT contain a literal material name, DO NOT call this tool — call plug-leak-by-hand instead. Inputs like 'plug the leak', 'stop the leak', 'seal the leak', 'fix the pipe' do NOT name a material — route those to plug-leak-by-hand.

customTools.$comment: DEFAULT leak tool. Carries the broad verb namespace ('plug', 'press', 'stop', 'cover', 'block', 'seal', 'fix') so ambiguous inputs route here, not to plug-leak-with-putty. Trigger leak-bare-hands emits the hint-free refusal.
customTools.description: DEFAULT tool for the maintenance-room leak when the player has NOT named anything to plug it with. Player tries to plug, press, stop, cover, block, seal, fix, or close the leak / pipe / hole — any phrasing that doesn't literally name a material. Examples: 'plug the leak', 'stop the water', 'press the hole', 'seal the leak', 'fix the pipe'. If the player names putty or any other material, use plug-leak-with-putty instead.

customTools.$comment: TURN the dam bolt WITH a named tool. Narrow tool: only fires when the player's input literally names the tool. DEFAULT for ALL other phrasings is turn-dam-bolt-by-hand (the bare-hand pair). Broad operating verbs ('turn', 'twist', 'open the gates') belong on turn-dam-bolt-by-hand — keep them OFF this description so the LLM doesn't match them here.
customTools.description: ONLY call when the player's input contains a literal tool name (e.g. 'turn bolt WITH wrench', 'use the wrench on the bolt'). If the input does NOT contain a literal tool name, DO NOT call this tool — call turn-dam-bolt-by-hand instead. Inputs like 'turn the bolt', 'twist bolt', 'open the gates', 'open the dam', 'release the gates' do NOT name a tool — route those to turn-dam-bolt-by-hand. NEVER auto-fill withItemId from inventory or context.

withItemId.description: the id of the tool the player literally named in their input (must come from the player's stated words — do not assume from inventory or context)

customTools.$comment: DEFAULT dam-bolt tool. Carries the broad verb namespace ('turn', 'twist', 'pull', 'pry', 'rotate', 'operate', 'open the gates/dam') so ambiguous inputs route here, not to turn-dam-bolt. Trigger dam-bolt-bare-hands emits the hint-free refusal.
customTools.description: DEFAULT tool for the dam control panel's iron bolt when the player has NOT named a tool. Player tries to turn, twist, pull, pry, rotate, operate, work, force, or otherwise act on the bolt / gates / dam — any phrasing that doesn't literally name a tool. Examples: 'turn the bolt', 'twist bolt', 'open the gates', 'open the dam', 'release the gates'. If the player names a tool (wrench, etc.), use turn-dam-bolt instead.

customTools.$comment: BOAT INFLATE WITH a named tool. Narrow tool: only fires when the player's input literally names the tool. DEFAULT for ALL other phrasings is inflate-boat-by-mouth. Broad verbs ('inflate', 'blow up', 'fill') belong on inflate-boat-by-mouth — keep them OFF this description. Boarding/disembarking are engine actions (not intents) — the LLM calls board(itemId) directly.
customTools.description: ONLY call when the player's input contains a literal tool name (e.g. 'inflate boat WITH pump', 'use the pump on the boat', 'pump up the raft'). If the input does NOT contain a literal tool name, DO NOT call this tool — call inflate-boat-by-mouth instead. Inputs like 'inflate the boat', 'blow up the boat', 'fill the raft', 'expand the boat' do NOT name a tool — route those to inflate-boat-by-mouth.

customTools.$comment: DEFAULT boat-inflation tool. Carries the broad verb namespace ('inflate', 'blow up', 'fill', 'expand', 'breathe into') so ambiguous inputs route here, not to inflate-boat. Trigger boat-bare-mouth emits the hint-free refusal.
customTools.description: DEFAULT tool for inflating the boat when the player has NOT named a tool. Player tries to inflate, blow up, fill, expand, breathe into, or open the boat / raft — any phrasing that doesn't literally name a tool (or that mentions breath/mouth/lungs). Examples: 'inflate the boat', 'blow up the boat', 'fill the raft', 'breathe into the boat', 'expand the raft'. If the player names the pump or any other tool, use inflate-boat instead.

customTools.$comment: BOAT REPAIR with putty (in tube). Active only when boat is punctured and player carries putty. Consumed on use.
customTools.description: Player plugs, repairs, patches, or applies putty to the punctured boat.

customTools.$comment: BOAT LAUNCH: shove the inflated boat off from the dam-base shore into the river. Player must be inside the inflated boat at dam-base. Backed by the boat-launches trigger which moves the boat (and the player riding it) to river-1, where the per-river drift triggers take over. With player-as-item, only the boat moves — the player rides along via container parentage.
customTools.description: Player launches, shoves off, casts off, releases, or pays out the boat into the river.
